meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Debugging with AI to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with project paralysis. Learn how to use AI debugging tools like Antigravity and Claude Code to turn broken code into a professional portfolio.
content.answer_block: Project paralysis stems from fear of the unknown. By leveraging AI-assisted debugging, you transform 'no project ideas' into an iterative learning loop. Use Antigravity to scaffold logic and Claude Code to execute real-time fixes, turning complex technical hurdles into manageable, portfolio-worthy milestones that prove your engineering capability to future employers.
content.intro:
The blank editor stares back at you—a digital void where your ambition meets the crushing weight of 'what should I build?' You’ve mastered the syntax, but you lack the map to turn that knowledge into a production-grade application. The paralysis isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of feedback loops. 

We are shifting the paradigm. Instead of chasing the 'perfect' idea, we’re going to embrace the 'broken' one. By using AI as your senior engineering partner, you will debug your way into existence, turning experimental failures into robust, impressive features that define your early career.

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope
steps.description: Identify a small, specific technical challenge that excites you. We use Antigravity to scope the architecture to prevent feature creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. I want to build a [insert idea, e.g., real-time task tracker]. Break this down into 3 core technical modules and define the data schema for the primary database model.

steps.title: Generate the Scaffold
steps.description: Use the AI to generate the boilerplate code. This removes the intimidation of the empty file.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the folder structure and boilerplate code for a [language/framework] project based on the schema defined in step 1. Include necessary dependencies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'scaffold project structure with necessary dependencies'

steps.title: Inject Intentional Bugs
steps.description: To learn debugging, you must break things. Introduce a logic error to test your understanding of the flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a logical error in the API handler that would cause a silent failure. Explain why this happens so I can learn to spot it.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'introduce a subtle race condition in the async data fetcher'

steps.title: Automated Root Cause Analysis
steps.description: Use Claude Code to trace the bug. Don't just fix it; analyze the execution path.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current codebase for potential bottlenecks. Provide a breakdown of the execution flow and identify where the error originated.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'why is the data fetch failing in the current implementation?'

steps.title: Iterative Patching
steps.description: Apply the fix and verify with automated tests. This builds the 'shippable' mindset.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a robust patch for the identified bug. Ensure the solution adheres to clean code principles and includes unit test coverage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply fix and run unit tests to verify resolution'

pro_tips: Treat your AI assistant as a junior-level peer; always review the code it generates for security vulnerabilities.
Use 'Explain like I'm a Senior Engineer' in your prompts to skip the basics and get straight to architectural patterns.
Keep a dedicated 'Debugging Log' file where you document every error fixed via AI to identify your personal coding blind spots.
Leverage Claude Code's terminal awareness to pipe logs directly into the chat for instant context-aware debugging.
Focus on 'shipping' over 'perfecting'; if the AI suggests a refactor, accept it only if you can explain it back in your own words.
content.next_steps: After completing this project, deploy it to a platform like Vercel or Railway and write a one-page technical post-mortem about the bugs you solved.
content.conversion_block: Your portfolio is waiting for the code you haven't written yet. Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start building the one in front of you. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
